inputs.price_source: [Assumption] Yahoo Finance BVERS last sale ~$2,300 (2026-04-10); bid/ask ~$2,375–$2,650; thin OTC Pink volume
inputs.fcf_source: FY2025 distribution $206.17 per unit (2025-12-31 Annual Report letter; 103.8% of net income)
inputs.shares_source: 2024 balance sheet: Limited partners 24,878 shares of interest; confirmed by net income / income per unit 2025 ($4,942,513 / $198.67)
inputs.book_per_share_caveat: Land carried at $3,511,890 historical cost (~$69/acre on ~50,622 timber acres); not fair-value NAV
inputs.dividend_source: FY2025 distribution per unit (annual report letter)
inputs.normalization_note: Lawrence uses FY2025 cash distribution as owner cash (not 2024's $305/unit payout funded by excess liquidity). Coal royalties cyclical; surface leasing share rising to 36.5% of revenue.
inputs.last_filing: FY2025 annual report via OTC Disclosure (amended 2026-04-28)

optionality_gate.floor_value_note: GAAP book ~$595/unit is accounting floor only; economic value is royalty stream + surface option. Cash $4.16M and $13.06M contracted minimum rentals (Note 6) bound liquidity risk.
optionality_gate.notes: Price ~4.0× GAAP book because land/coal reserves carried at historical cost; stance gate uses Lawrence distribution path, not book discount.

gaap_vs_fair_value.policy_source: Note 4: Land $3,511,890 at historical cost; Reserve Grid based on independent appraisals (not fully marked on balance sheet)

not_in_model_requires_refresh: Independent appraisal dollar values for coal/timber reserves (grid shows volumes only)
Per-acre fair marks on ~40,000 coal-bearing acres and ~9,600 non-coal acres
Private-market unit transaction comps beyond thin OTC prints

segments_or_options.label: Metallurgical coal reserves (~22.1M tons YE 2025)
segments_or_options.nav_assumption: [Assumption] Partial mark above GAAP land line; reserve grid tons only, no filing dollar NAV
segments_or_options.evidence: BCC Reserve Grid 2025; coal royalties $4.37M FY2025

segments_or_options.label: Surface leasing, hospitality, commercial ground leases
segments_or_options.evidence: Rentals and other revenue $2.85M (+7.5% YoY); 36.5% of total revenue FY2025

segments_or_options.label: Undeveloped / non-producing coal acreage and timber growth
segments_or_options.evidence: ~40,000 coal-bearing acres + timber growth 4,255 MBF in 2025; management surface land-lessor plan

segments_or_options.label: GP consent / non-reporting LP transfer restriction
segments_or_options.base_terminal_rationale: Market structure discount is ongoing, not a separate terminal option; captured in yield lens
segments_or_options.evidence: 2022-10-28 Supplemental Information; unit transfer requires GP consent

bear.notes: Sustained met coal price weakness; distributions cut toward net income; two-customer concentration

base.notes: FY2025 distribution $206/unit; surface leasing mix rises; coal royalties mid-cycle

lines.label: Met coal reserves (overlay only)
lines.math: [Assumption] 22.1M tons metallurgical reserves; not on balance sheet at fair value

lines.label: Royalty + surface cash-flow franchise
lines.math: Implied by market price $2,400 vs GAAP $595 + overlays; not additive to Lawrence cash path

sotp_build.notes: Market price embeds royalty franchise; overlay lines illustrate GAAP vs economic gap only

bull.notes: Met coal up-cycle + surface development (Dayton St., Cabins at Pine Haven) accelerates rentals

option_scan.question: GAAP book misstates core assets?
option_scan.evidence: Land $3.5M at historical cost on ~50,552 acres; coal/timber reserves appraised in grid but not fair-valued on BS (Note 4)

option_scan.question: Undeveloped reserves / acreage / dormant royalty?
option_scan.evidence: 22.1M tons met coal reserves; ~40,000 coal-bearing acres; minimum rental credit $937,930 (Note 6)

option_scan.question: In-business loss segment?
option_scan.evidence: Operating income $4.89M FY2025; fixed-cost royalty model

option_scan.question: Backlog / contracted revenue not in FCF?
Private or illiquid stakes?
option_scan.evidence: Wholly owned mini-storage (91%) and Woodlands Amenities subsidiaries consolidated

option_scan.question: Embedded product option already in revenue?
option_scan.evidence: Surface leasing growth already in rentals line; coal royalties remain commodity-linked

paths.label: Bear scenario (Lawrence)
paths.weight_why: Downside stress; low weight because bear is a sensitivity, not a competing explanation of base mechanics.
weight_falsifiers: Bear assumptions become base case in filings (e.g. sustained volume collapse)
paths.hard_to_vary: yes: asymmetric low weight is structural

paths.label: Bull scenario (Lawrence)
paths.weight_why: Upside stress probe; higher than bear because optionality archetype has skew, but still below primary paths.
weight_falsifiers: Bull growth sustained in filings for 4+ quarters without falsifier trigger

deutsch_checks.reach: Weights encode evidence tier (A primary → D external), not fitted to hit a target IRR

synthesis.notes: Capstone IRR combining filings, segments, overlays, third-party, qualitative

context_overlay.disclaimer: Context only. Tailwinds inform stance and overlay sizing; they do not auto-inflate Lawrence base IRR. Promotion to base case requires [HUMAN REVIEW].

lens_consensus.disclaimer: Advisory lens blend only. Does not replace Lawrence total-synthesis stance gate without [HUMAN REVIEW].

human_review.notes: Human approved watch stance, synthesis weights, governance gate, and monitoring items 2026-06-11. OTC price ~$2,400 retained as sizing assumption; post-2030 renewal and related-party fees tracked as ongoing monitors, not stance blockers.
items_resolved: Synthesis weights and qualitative pp auto-build approved
Gate 4 aligned management: acceptable for watch sizing with GP fee disclosure
Unit price ~$2,400 assumption approved for model; refresh on accumulate
Two-customer concentration and related-party fees: disclosed; sized for watch only
